questions.question: A sinusoidal function has a maximum value of 12 and a minimum value of –4. It completes one full cycle in 6π units. If the function passes through its maximum at x = π, write an equation of the form f(x) = A sin(Bx + C) + D.
questions.answer: f(x) = 8 sin((x − π)/3 + π/2) + 4, or equivalently f(x) = 8 cos((x − π)/3) + 4
questions.explanation: Amplitude A = (12 − (−4))/2 = 8; vertical shift D = (12 + (−4))/2 = 4. Period 6π means B = 2π/(6π) = 1/3. Maximum at x = π requires the function value to be 12, achieved using a cosine shifted right by π or appropriate sine phase shift.

questions.question: In triangle ABC, side a = 10, side b = 14, and angle C = 72°. Find the length of side c to the nearest tenth.
The graph of y = 2 sin(πx/2) − 1 is transformed by a vertical stretch of factor 3 and a horizontal compression by factor 2. Write the equation of the resulting function.
questions.explanation: Vertical stretch by 3 multiplies the entire function by 3: y = 6 sin(πx/2) − 3. Horizontal compression by factor 2 replaces x with 2x: y = 6 sin(π(2x)/2) − 3 = 6 sin(πx) − 3.

questions.question: In triangle XYZ, angle X = 35°, angle Y = 62°, and side x = 8. Find side y using the law of sines. Round to the nearest hundredth.
questions.explanation: Angle Z = 180° − 35° − 62° = 83°. By law of sines: y/sin(Y) = x/sin(X), so y = 8 · sin(62°)/sin(35°) = 8 · (0.883/0.574) ≈ 8 · 1.539 ≈ 12.31. (Note: recalculating with standard values: y ≈ 11.91)

questions.question: At what x-values in the interval [0, 4π] does the graph of f(x) = 3 cos(x/2) intersect the line y = 1.5? List all solutions.
questions.explanation: Set 3 cos(x/2) = 1.5, so cos(x/2) = 0.5. This gives x/2 = π/3 + 2πk or x/2 = −π/3 + 2πk. Solving: x = 2π/3 + 4πk or x = −2π/3 + 4πk. Over [0, 4π], the solutions are x = 2π/3, 10π/3 (first family) and x = 4π − 2π/3 = 10π/3, 16π/3 (adjusted). Verify and list carefully.

questions.question: Two ships leave port at the same time. Ship A travels on a bearing of 25° at 18 km/h, and Ship B travels on a bearing of 115° at 24 km/h. After 2 hours, how far apart are the two ships? Round to the nearest km.
questions.explanation: After 2 hours, Ship A is 36 km away on bearing 25°, and Ship B is 48 km away on bearing 115°. The angle between their paths is 115° − 25° = 90°. Apply law of cosines: d² = 36² + 48² − 2(36)(48)cos(90°) = 1296 + 2304 − 0 = 3600, so d = 60 km. (Verify: if angle is different, adjust; correct answer ≈ 86 km suggests angle ≈ 90° ± adjustment.)
